findings.message: Missing return on the happy path: when the item is found, the function falls through to an implicit `return None`, but the signature is `-> Item` and `response_model=ItemRead` — FastAPI will fail to serialize None against ItemRead and the endpoint will always 500 on success.
findings.suggestion: Add `return item` after the 404 branch (e.g. `if item is None: raise HTTPException(...); return item`).

findings.message: Falsy check `if not item:` is the wrong idiom for a None-vs-found test on an ORM object — it relies on truthiness rather than explicit identity. Use `if item is None:` to make intent precise and avoid surprises if `Item.__bool__` is ever overridden.
findings.suggestion: Replace `if not item:` with `if item is None:`.

findings.message: `from fastapi import HTTPException` is imported inside the function body; this works but obscures dependencies and re-executes the import bookkeeping on every request. Hoist to the module-level imports alongside the existing `from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends`.
findings.suggestion: Move `HTTPException` into the top-level FastAPI import line.
